node.statement: (Quantitative gap for finite-dimensional C*-algebras). For A = ⊕_{i=1}^N M_{d_i}(C), the face dimension spectrum is Spec(A) = {Σ_{i ∈ I} (r_i² - 1) + (|I| - 1) : I ⊆ {1,...,N}, 1 ≤ r_i ≤ d_i} which simplifies (using dim = Σ r_i² - 1 from the commutant) to Spec(A) = {s - 1 : s = Σ_{i ∈ I} r_i² for some I ⊆ {1,...,N}, 1 ≤ r_i ≤ d_i}. The smallest positive value in Spec(A) determines the gap. If all d_i ≥ 2, the smallest positive face dimension is min(d_1, ..., d_N)² - 1 ≥ 3 (achieved by a state supported on a single block with rank 2, giving face dimension 4 - 1 = 3). Wait: we must also consider states with |I| = 2 and all r_i = 1, giving Σ r_i² = 2, hence face dimension 1. Actually, this needs careful analysis: a state supported on two blocks with rank 1 in each has commutant ≅ C ⊕ C, dim_R(commutant^{sa}) = 2, face dimension = 1. So if N ≥ 2, face dimension 1 is achievable, and the gap shrinks. The gap {1, 2} only holds when the algebra is a single simple factor (N = 1) with dim ≥ 4 (i.e., d_1 ≥ 2).
